Toggle navigation Phaeomoniella chlamydospora (PHMOCH) Menu Overview → Documents Overview Code created in: 2007-03-10 Basic information EPPO Code: PHMOCH Preferred name: Phaeomoniella chlamydospora Authority: (W.Gams, Crous, M.J.Wingfield & Mugnai) Crous & W.Gams Other scientific names Name Authority Phaeoacremonium chlamydosporum W.Gams, Crous, M.J.Wingfield & Mugnai Common names Name Language esca of grapevine English petri decline of grapevine English esca de la vigne French Propose photo Taxonomy Kingdom Fungi      (
      1FUNGK      ) Phylum Ascomycota      (
      1ASCOP      ) Subphylum Pezizomycotina      (
      1PEZIQ      ) Class Eurotiomycetes      (
      1EUROC      ) Subclass Chaetothyriomycetidae      (
      1KHAEL      ) Order Phaeomoniellales      (
      1PHAEO      ) Family Phaeomoniellaceae      (
      1PHAOF      ) Genus Phaeomoniella      (
      1PHMOG      ) Species Phaeomoniella chlamydospora      (
      PHMOCH      )

seed.symptoms: Interveinal chlorosis and necrosis creating 'tiger stripe' pattern on leaves; dark streaking inside woody tissue; fruit with dark spots.
seed.causes: Wood-rotting fungi complex including Phaeomoniella chlamydospora and Phaeoacremonium species; enters via pruning wounds.
seed.treatment: No fully curative treatment; wound protectants (sodium arsenite banned in EU); remove and burn affected wood.
seed.prevention: Protect pruning wounds with fungicide paste; prune during dry weather; avoid large wounds.
